content.problemStatement:
A string is said to be using strong language if it contains at least KKK consecutive characters '*'.

You are given a string SSS with length NNN. Determine whether it uses strong language or not.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 10^6
S contains only lowercase English letters and '*'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line has two integers N and K, the length of the string and the required number of consecutive '*'. The second line contains the string S of length N, consisting of lowercase letters and the character '*'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if S contains at least K consecutive '*' characters, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: No two consecutive '*', so answer is NO. Test case 2: There are two adjacent '*', so answer is YES. Test case 3: No '*' at all, so answer is NO.
First string has three consecutive '*', which is >=2, so YES. Second string's longest run of '*' is 2, which is <4, so NO.
K=1, any single '*' satisfies the condition, thus YES.

hidden.label: edge case with K=N but no '*'

solution.approach: For each test case scan the string from left to right while maintaining a counter of the current run of consecutive '*'. Whenever a non-'*' character is encountered, reset the counter to zero. If the counter ever reaches K, we can stop scanning and answer "YES". If the end of the string is reached without the counter reaching K, answer "NO". This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the string. Overall O(sum of N) ≤ 10^6.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input string.
